name: Courier Chess
author: Medieval (c. 1200) / AGP port
description: Courier Chess (Kurierspiel), a medieval German chess variant played c. 1200-1800 on a 12x8 board. It introduced the Courier, which moves exactly like the modern bishop (an unlimited diagonal slider) -- roughly three centuries before this move entered standard chess. Each side has a King, a Queen (the medieval Ferz: a one-step diagonal mover), two Rooks, two Knights, two Bishops/Alfils (the (2,2) elephant-leaper of Shatranj), two Couriers (modern bishops), a Mann or Sage (moves like a king but is not royal), a Schleich or Wazir (steps one square orthogonally), and twelve pawns on the third rank. Pawns step a single square (no double step, no en passant) and promote only to a Ferz on the last rank. There is no castling. Checkmate wins; stalemate is a draw.
